steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says Kepler-296e is too far to reach. The next says humanity should focus on our solar system. The blank must signal a shift from an impractical option to a different course of action.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Likewise,’: signals similarity or parallel ideas — but the two sentences contrast, not agree. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Instead,’: signals a turn from an impractical course of action, trying to reach Kepler-296e, to an alternative course, focusing on our solar system. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘For example,’: frames the second sentence as evidence or illustration, not a new direction. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Next,’: implies sequential steps, not a pivot to an alternative. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
